title: Gooey Egyptian Squash Bake
description: A floral and indulgent Egyptian-inspired dish featuring roasted squash, creamy beurre monte, and a hint of spices. Perfect for a romantic date night dinner.
instructions: Peel, de-seed, and chop the acorn squash into 1-inch cubes. Place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Roast the squash in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until tender and lightly browned.
In a medium saucepan, melt the unsalted butter over medium heat.
Add the granulated and brown sugars, ground cinnamon, and ground ginger to the saucepan. Whisk until smooth.
Add the whole milk to the saucepan, whisking continuously until the mixture thickens and comes to a simmer.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and a pinch of kosher salt.
Stir the eggs into the saucepan with the milk mixture, cooking for 2-3 minutes or until the mixture thickens and coats the back of a spoon.
Remove the squash from the oven and let cool slightly.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the roasted squash, beurre monte mixture, and chopped fresh parsley (optional).
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and smooth the top.
Bake for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the filling is set.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 10-15 minutes before serving.
Serve warm, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
To make the beurre monte, melt 1/4 cup (60g) of unsalted butter in a small saucepan over low heat.
Slowly pour in 1 cup (240ml) of heavy cream, whisking continuously until the mixture thickens and comes to a simmer.
Continue cooking for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the beurre monte has doubled in volume and is smooth and creamy.
